skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X actions through the inference.sh belt CLI, including posts, media posts, likes, retweets, DMs, follows, deletes, and profile lookups.
skill.summary: Run Twitter/X posting and engagement workflows with the inference.sh belt CLI.
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: Static external command and network findings are true positives because the skill instructs users to run belt CLI commands that call inference.sh and Twitter/X services. The weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 29 are false positives from prose and a Markdown table, with no cryptographic code present. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found, but the skill can perform authenticated public and private social media actions.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External CLI Social Media Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill tells users to run belt commands that can post, like, retweet, send DMs, follow users, get profiles, and delete posts on Twitter/X. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but mistakes or unsafe prompts can cause public account actions or private messages.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and map to authenticated Twitter/X side effects. The risk is operational misuse rather than confirmed malware.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Network Service Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill depends on inference.sh, Twitter/X integrations, external media URLs, and related app services. Content, media URLs, account identifiers, and message text may be sent to remote services when users run the examples.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Markdown directly references external services and media URLs. This confirms network dependence, though it matches the advertised social automation use case.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3 and 29. Line 3 is descriptive text about the X API, and line 29 is a Markdown table header; neither contains cryptographic implementation or hash usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible prose and Markdown structure, not executable cryptographic code. No weak cryptographic operation is present in this single-file skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples for External CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides shell command examples for belt CLI execution. Commands are concrete and expected for this skill, but they still perform external operations under the user account.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file contains multiple bash blocks with belt commands. There is no evidence of command injection, but external command execution is central to the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded External URLs
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes inference.sh documentation links, an externally hosted image, and an example media URL. These links are expected, but users should review any external media before posting it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URLs are plainly present in Markdown links and examples. They appear documentation-oriented, not hidden exfiltration endpoints.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable ways to publish and manage Twitter/X activity from an AI coding workflow. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear belt CLI steps for posting, engagement, DMs, follows, and profile lookups.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to authenticate with the inference.sh belt CLI.
Posts text tweets through the x/post-tweet app.
Creates Twitter/X posts with media URLs through x/post-create.
Likes, retweets, deletes, and retrieves posts by tweet ID.
Sends direct messages and follows users through listed X apps.
Combines AI image or video generation with a follow-up Twitter/X post.
limitations: Requires the belt CLI and a configured inference.sh account.
Requires a connected Twitter/X integration before account actions work.
Does not verify message consent, platform policy compliance, or brand review.
Examples rely on remote services and may create public or private account changes.

use_cases.title: Publish Launch Updates
use_cases.description: Draft and post concise release updates from a terminal-based AI workflow.

use_cases.title: Manage Engagement Tasks
use_cases.description: Like, retweet, follow, or inspect specific Twitter/X accounts and posts with explicit IDs.

use_cases.title: Post Generated Media
use_cases.description: Generate image or video assets with inference.sh apps and publish them with a prepared caption.

prompt_templates.title: Post a Simple Tweet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use twitter-automation to post this approved text to Twitter/X: [paste text]. Confirm the action before running the command.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Media Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Post with an existing image or video URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Twitter/X post with this caption and media URL. Show the final text and ask for approval before using x/post-create.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Post Before Engagement
prompt_templates.scenario: Engage with a known tweet ID
prompt_templates.prompt: Get details for this tweet ID, summarize the result, then ask whether to like, retweet, or take no action.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Generated Media Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a media asset from this prompt, prepare a Twitter/X caption, and post only after I approve the final content and media URL.

output_examples.input: Post an approved launch update to Twitter/X.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares the tweet text, confirms the account action, runs the posting workflow, and reports the posted result.

output_examples.input: Create a post using a provided image URL and caption.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks the caption and media URL, asks for approval, then creates a media post through the listed X app.

output_examples.input: Look up a tweet and decide whether to retweet it.
output_examples.output: The assistant retrieves tweet details, summarizes the content, and waits for explicit approval before any engagement action.

best_practices: Confirm every public post, deletion, follow, retweet, like, and direct message before running a command.
Use approved copy, verified media URLs, and clear account ownership before publishing.
Keep credentials and integration setup in the belt CLI, not inside prompts or shared files.
anti_patterns: Do not automate bulk follows, mass DMs, or engagement spam.
Do not post unreviewed AI-generated text or media to a production account.
Do not paste access tokens, private account credentials, or customer data into prompts.

faq.question: Does this skill post directly to Twitter/X?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides belt CLI commands for Twitter/X actions after the user has configured the required integration.

faq.question: Can it post images or videos?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill shows x/post-create examples that use media URLs for image or video posts.

faq.question: Does it include scheduling logic?
faq.answer: No. The description mentions content scheduling, but the visible file only shows immediate CLI workflows.

faq.question: Is approval recommended before each action?
faq.answer: Yes. Public posts, DMs, deletions, follows, likes, and retweets should require explicit user approval.

faq.question: Can it read Twitter/X profiles or posts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill lists commands for getting a user profile and getting tweet details by ID.

faq.question: What accounts or services are required?
faq.answer: Users need the inference.sh belt CLI and a configured Twitter/X integration for authenticated actions.
